1→# Configuration Reference
     2→
     3→Complete reference for all YAML configuration options.
     4→
     5→## Required Fields
     6→
     7→### `name`
     8→
     9→**Type:** `string` (alphanumeric and underscores only)
    10→
    11→The name of the API being generated. Used as:
    12→- Output subdirectory name
    13→- Namespace identifier
    14→- Part of operation IDs
    15→
    16→```yaml
    17→name: myapi
    18→```
    19→
    20→### `ontologies`
    21→
    22→**Type:** `list of URI/path strings`
    23→
    24→List of ontology files to load. Multiple files are merged into a single ontology.
    25→
    26→```yaml
    27→ontologies:
    28→  - ./my-ontology.owl
    29→  - ./extensions.owl
    30→```
    31→
    32→Supports:
    33→- Relative paths (resolved from config file location)
    34→- Absolute paths
    35→- URIs (`file://`, `http://`, `https://`)
    36→
    37→### `outputDir`
    38→
    39→**Type:** `path`
    40→
    41→Directory where generated files will be written. A subdirectory named after `name` will be created.
    42→
    43→```yaml
    44→outputDir: ./output
    45→```
    46→
    47→Output structure:
    48→```
    49→output/myapi/
    50→├── effective-config.yaml
    51→├── ontology0.owl
    52→├── ontology-flat.owl
    53→├── ontology-merged.owl
    54→├── openapi.json
    55→└── openapi.yaml
    56→```
    57→
    58→## Optional Fields
    59→
    60→### `extends`
    61→
    62→**Type:** `path`
    63→
    64→Path to another configuration file to extend. Fields are merged recursively.
    65→
    66→```yaml
    67→extends: ../base-config.yaml
    68→```
    69→
    70→See [Configuration Overview](index.md) for merge behavior details.
    71→
    72→### `rootClass`
    73→
    74→**Type:** `IRI string`
    75→
    76→The IRI of the root class that defines the API resources. Required if multiple classes are subclasses of `APIRoot`.
    77→
    78→```yaml
    79→rootClass: https://example.com/ontology/MyAPIV1.0
    80→```
    81→
    82→If not specified, ASG infers the root from the single class that is a subclass of `APIRoot`.
    83→
    84→### `catalogFile`
    85→
    86→**Type:** `path`
    87→
    88→Path to an XML catalog file for resolving ontology imports.
    89→
    90→```yaml
    91→catalogFile: ./catalog-v001.xml
    92→```
    93→
    94→### `enforceRequiredness`
    95→
    96→**Type:** `boolean`
    97→**Default:** `false`
    98→
    99→When `true`, properties with cardinality restrictions (`minCardinality >= 1`, `exactCardinality >= 1`, or `someValuesFrom`) are added to the `required` array in the JSON Schema.
   100→
   101→```yaml
   102→enforceRequiredness: true
   103→```
   104→
   105→### `namespaceMappings`
   106→
   107→**Type:** `map` or `list`
   108→
   109→Defines how IRIs are flattened into a single namespace. Values are regex patterns to match source namespaces.
   110→
   111→**As a list** (target namespace inferred from first ontology):
   112→
   113→```yaml
   114→namespaceMappings:
   115→  - "!http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#.*"     # Exclude OWL namespace
   116→  - "!http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#.*"  # Exclude XSD namespace
   117→  - ".*"                                      # Include everything else
   118→```
   119→
   120→**As a map** (explicit target namespace):
   121→
   122→```yaml
   123→namespaceMappings:
   124→  https://example.com/api/:
   125→    - https://example.com/.*
   126→    - https://other.com/.*
   127→```
   128→
   129→Patterns prefixed with `!` are exclusions.
   130→
   131→### `securitySchemeName`
   132→
   133→**Type:** `string`
   134→
   135→Name of the security scheme to use. If not specified, the first OAuth2 scheme found in the OpenAPI template is used.
   136→
   137→```yaml
   138→securitySchemeName: oauth2
   139→```
   140→
   141→Supports placeholders: `{api}`, `{action}`, `{model}`, `{security}`
   142→
   143→### `defaultSecurityTemplate`
   144→
   145→**Type:** `string`
   146→
   147→Template for generating security requirements when no `security` annotation is present on a resource.
   148→
   149→```yaml
   150→defaultSecurityTemplate: "{api}:{model}:{action}"
   151→```
   152→
   153→**Placeholders:**
   154→- `{api}` - The API name from config
   155→- `{action}` - HTTP method mapped via `securityActionMappings`
   156→- `{model}` - The resource name
   157→
   158→Set to `null` or empty to suppress security generation for unannotated resources.
   159→
   160→### `securityTemplate`
   161→
   162→**Type:** `string`
   163→
   164→Template for generating security requirements when a `security` annotation IS present.
   165→
   166→```yaml
   167→securityTemplate: "{security}"
   168→```
   169→
   170→**Placeholders:**
   171→- `{security}` - The value from the `security` annotation
   172→- `{api}`, `{action}`, `{model}` - Same as above
   173→
   174→### `securityActionMappings`
   175→
   176→**Type:** `map`
   177→
   178→Maps HTTP methods to action names for use in security templates.
   179→
   180→```yaml
   181→securityActionMappings:
   182→  GET: read
   183→  POST: create
   184→  PUT: update
   185→  PATCH: update
   186→  DELETE: delete
   187→```
   188→
   189→### `templates`
   190→
   191→**Type:** `object`
   192→
   193→OpenAPI template objects. See [Templates](templates.md) for details.
   194→
   195→```yaml
   196→templates:
   197→  openapi:
   198→    openapi: 3.1.0
   199→    info:
   200→      title: My API
   201→      version: 1.0.0
   202→  collectionPathItem:
   203→    # PathItem template for collection endpoints
   204→  singlePathItem:
   205→    # PathItem template for single-resource endpoints
   206→```
   207→
   208→## Complete Example
   209→
   210→```yaml
   211→extends: base-config.yaml
   212→
   213→name: employment_api
   214→
   215→ontologies:
   216→  - ${configDir}/ontologies/employment.owl
   217→  - ${configDir}/ontologies/extensions.owl
   218→
   219→outputDir: ${configDir}/../../output
   220→
   221→catalogFile: ${configDir}/ontologies/catalog.xml
   222→
   223→rootClass: https://example.com/employment/EmploymentAPIV1.0
   224→
   225→enforceRequiredness: true
   226→
   227→namespaceMappings:
   228→  https://example.com/employment/:
   229→    - "!http://www.w3.org/.*"
   230→    - ".*"
   231→
   232→securitySchemeName: oauth2
   233→defaultSecurityTemplate: "{api}.{model}.{action}"
   234→securityActionMappings:
   235→  GET: read
   236→  POST: create
   237→  PUT: update
   238→  PATCH: update
   239→  DELETE: delete
   240→
   241→templates:
   242→  openapi:
   243→    openapi: 3.1.0
   244→    info:
   245→      title: Employment API
   246→      version: 1.0.0
   247→      description: HR management API
   248→      contact:
   249→        name: API Support
   250→        email: support@example.com
   251→    servers:
   252→      - url: https://api.example.com/v1
   253→        description: Production
   254→    components:
   255→      securitySchemes:
   256→        oauth2:
   257→          type: oauth2
   258→          flows:
   259→            authorizationCode:
   260→              authorizationUrl: https://auth.example.com/authorize
   261→              tokenUrl: https://auth.example.com/token
   262→```
   263→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

# Configuration Reference

Complete reference for all YAML configuration options.

## Required Fields

### `name`

**Type:** `string` (alphanumeric and underscores only)

The name of the API being generated. Used as:
- Output subdirectory name
- Namespace identifier
- Part of operation IDs

```yaml
name: myapi
```

### `ontologies`

**Type:** `list of URI/path strings`

List of ontology files to load. Multiple files are merged into a single ontology.

```yaml
ontologies:
  - ./my-ontology.owl
  - ./extensions.owl
```

Supports:
- Relative paths (resolved from config file location)
- Absolute paths
- URIs (`file://`, `http://`, `https://`)

### `outputDir`

**Type:** `path`

Directory where generated files will be written. A subdirectory named after `name` will be created.

```yaml
outputDir: ./output
```

Output structure:
```
output/myapi/
├── effective-config.yaml
├── ontology0.owl
├── ontology-flat.owl
├── ontology-merged.owl
├── openapi.json
└── openapi.yaml
```

## Optional Fields

### `extends`

**Type:** `path`

Path to another configuration file to extend. Fields are merged recursively.

```yaml
extends: ../base-config.yaml
```

See [Configuration Overview](index.md) for merge behavior details.

### `rootClass`

**Type:** `IRI string`

The IRI of the root class that defines the API resources. Required if multiple classes are subclasses of `APIRoot`.

```yaml
rootClass: https://example.com/ontology/MyAPIV1.0
```

If not specified, ASG infers the root from the single class that is a subclass of `APIRoot`.

### `catalogFile`

**Type:** `path`

Path to an XML catalog file for resolving ontology imports.

```yaml
catalogFile: ./catalog-v001.xml
```

### `enforceRequiredness`

**Type:** `boolean`
**Default:** `false`

When `true`, properties with cardinality restrictions (`minCardinality >= 1`, `exactCardinality >= 1`, or `someValuesFrom`) are added to the `required` array in the JSON Schema.

```yaml
enforceRequiredness: true
```

### `namespaceMappings`

**Type:** `map` or `list`

Defines how IRIs are flattened into a single namespace. Values are regex patterns to match source namespaces.

**As a list** (target namespace inferred from first ontology):

```yaml
namespaceMappings:
  - "!http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#.*"     # Exclude OWL namespace
  - "!http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#.*"  # Exclude XSD namespace
  - ".*"                                      # Include everything else
```

**As a map** (explicit target namespace):

```yaml
namespaceMappings:
  https://example.com/api/:
    - https://example.com/.*
    - https://other.com/.*
```

Patterns prefixed with `!` are exclusions.

### `securitySchemeName`

**Type:** `string`

Name of the security scheme to use. If not specified, the first OAuth2 scheme found in the OpenAPI template is used.

```yaml
securitySchemeName: oauth2
```

Supports placeholders: `{api}`, `{action}`, `{model}`, `{security}`

### `defaultSecurityTemplate`

**Type:** `string`

Template for generating security requirements when no `security` annotation is present on a resource.

```yaml
defaultSecurityTemplate: "{api}:{model}:{action}"
```

**Placeholders:**
- `{api}` - The API name from config
- `{action}` - HTTP method mapped via `securityActionMappings`
- `{model}` - The resource name

Set to `null` or empty to suppress security generation for unannotated resources.

### `securityTemplate`

**Type:** `string`

Template for generating security requirements when a `security` annotation IS present.

```yaml
securityTemplate: "{security}"
```

**Placeholders:**
- `{security}` - The value from the `security` annotation
- `{api}`, `{action}`, `{model}` - Same as above

### `securityActionMappings`

**Type:** `map`

Maps HTTP methods to action names for use in security templates.

```yaml
securityActionMappings:
  GET: read
  POST: create
  PUT: update
  PATCH: update
  DELETE: delete
```

### `templates`

**Type:** `object`

OpenAPI template objects. See [Templates](templates.md) for details.

```yaml
templates:
  openapi:
    openapi: 3.1.0
    info:
      title: My API
      version: 1.0.0
  collectionPathItem:
    # PathItem template for collection endpoints
  singlePathItem:
    # PathItem template for single-resource endpoints
```

## Complete Example

```yaml
extends: base-config.yaml

name: employment_api

ontologies:
  - ${configDir}/ontologies/employment.owl
  - ${configDir}/ontologies/extensions.owl

outputDir: ${configDir}/../../output

catalogFile: ${configDir}/ontologies/catalog.xml

rootClass: https://example.com/employment/EmploymentAPIV1.0

enforceRequiredness: true

namespaceMappings:
  https://example.com/employment/:
    - "!http://www.w3.org/.*"
    - ".*"

securitySchemeName: oauth2
defaultSecurityTemplate: "{api}.{model}.{action}"
securityActionMappings:
  GET: read
  POST: create
  PUT: update
  PATCH: update
  DELETE: delete

templates:
  openapi:
    openapi: 3.1.0
    info:
      title: Employment API
      version: 1.0.0
      description: HR management API
      contact:
        name: API Support
        email: support@example.com
    servers:
      - url: https://api.example.com/v1
        description: Production
    components:
      securitySchemes:
        oauth2:
          type: oauth2
          flows:
            authorizationCode:
              authorizationUrl: https://auth.example.com/authorize
              tokenUrl: https://auth.example.com/token
```

input.new_string:
## Documentation Updates

### 1. Config Reference: docs/configuration/config-reference.md

Add new section under "Optional Fields":

```markdown
### `maxIndividualDepth`

**Type:** `integer`
**Default:** `2`

Controls how deeply object property assertions on Pattern B taxonomy individuals are traversed when generating `oneOf` schemas.

```yaml
maxIndividualDepth: 2
```

| Value | Behavior |
|-------|----------|
| `0` | Legacy mode: generates simple string enum (backward compatible) |
| `1` | Single level: only direct property assertions on individuals |
| `2+` | Recursive: expands nested individuals up to specified depth |

**Example with depth 2:**

OWL individual with nested object property:
```turtle
sensor:SensorTypeAnalog a sensor:SensorTypeCode ;
    skos:notation "analog" ;
    sensor:hasCategory sensor:MeasurementCategory .

sensor:MeasurementCategory a owl:NamedIndividual ;
    skos:notation "measurement" ;
    sensor:unit "volts" .
```

Generates `oneOf` with nested objects:
```yaml
oneOf:
  - type: object
    properties:
      code:
        const: "analog"
        type: string
      hasCategory:
        type: object
        properties:
          code:
            const: "measurement"
            type: string
          unit:
            const: "volts"
            type: string
```
```

### 2. Taxonomy Patterns: docs/ontology-design/taxonomy-patterns.md

Update the Pattern B section. Replace the current "OpenAPI Output" showing simple enum with:

```markdown
### OpenAPI Output

With `maxIndividualDepth >= 1` (default), Pattern B generates `oneOf` schemas with const objects:

```yaml
hasSensorType:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    oneOf:
      - type: object
        properties:
          code:
            const: "analog"
            type: string
          displayName:
            const: "Analog"
            type: string
          definition:
            const: "Analog sensor type."
            type: string
      - type: object
        properties:
          code:
            const: "digital"
            type: string
          displayName:
            const: "Digital"
            type: string
          definition:
            const: "Digital sensor type."
            type: string
```

### Object Property Assertions

When Pattern B individuals have object property assertions, these are expanded as nested objects (up to `maxIndividualDepth`):

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeAnalog">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/SensorTypeCode"/>
    <skos:notation>analog</skos:notation>
    <example:hasCategory rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementCategory"/>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
```

**OpenAPI (depth 2):**
```yaml
oneOf:
  - type: object
    properties:
      code:
        const: "analog"
        type: string
      hasCategory:
        type: object
        properties:
          code:
            const: "measurement"
            type: string
```

### Cycle Detection

If object property assertions form a cycle (A → B → A), ASG detects this and uses a string reference instead of infinite nesting.

### Legacy Mode

Set `maxIndividualDepth: 0` to generate simple string enums (pre-enhancement behavior):

```yaml
hasSensorType:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    enum:
      - analog
      - digital
```
```

Also update the Pattern Overview table at the top:

```markdown
| Pattern | OWL Structure | OpenAPI Output | Use Case |
|---------|---------------|----------------|----------|
| **A** | `owl:oneOf` with untyped individuals | Inline string enum | Simple, closed enumeration |
| **B** | `owl:oneOf` with typed individuals | `oneOf` with const objects | Closed enumeration with graphs |
| **C** | Named class (no SKOS) | `$ref` to schema | Extensible, hierarchical taxonomy |
| **D** | SKOS-based class | Inline string enum | Standards-compliant vocabulary |
```

Update the Pattern A vs B Comparison table:

```markdown
| Aspect | Pattern A | Pattern B |
|--------|-----------|-----------|
| Individual typing | `owl:NamedIndividual` only | Domain class + metadata |
| OpenAPI output | Simple string enum | `oneOf` with const objects |
| Property assertions | Not supported | Expanded as nested objects |
| SPARQL query | Parse `owl:oneOf` | `?x a SensorTypeCode` |
| Metadata support | `rdfs:label` only | Full annotation + property support |
```

## Test Updates

1→# Plan: Enhance Pattern B to Support Individual Graphs
     2→
     3→## Summary
     4→
     5→Enhance Pattern B taxonomy handling to support individuals with object property assertions, generating `oneOf` schemas with `const` objects instead of simple string enums.
     6→
     7→## Requirements
     8→
     9→| Decision | Choice |
    10→|----------|--------|
    11→| Output structure | `oneOf` with `const` objects |
    12→| Nesting depth | Configurable via `maxIndividualDepth`, default 2 |
    13→| Data properties | Include as `const` with `type` |
    14→| Code/identifier | Fallback: `skos:notation` → IRI local name |
    15→| Nested individuals | Expand to nested objects recursively (up to max depth) |
    16→
    17→## Example Transformation
    18→
    19→**OWL Input:**
    20→```turtle
    21→sensor:SensorTypeAnalog a owl:NamedIndividual, sensor:SensorTypeCode ;
    22→    skos:notation "analog" ;
    23→    skos:prefLabel "Analog Sensor" ;
    24→    sensor:hasCategory sensor:MeasurementCategory ;
    25→    sensor:voltage "5V" .
    26→
    27→sensor:MeasurementCategory a owl:NamedIndividual ;
    28→    skos:notation "measurement" ;
    29→    sensor:unit "volts" .
    30→```
    31→
    32→**OpenAPI Output (maxIndividualDepth: 2):**
    33→```yaml
    34→oneOf:
    35→  - type: object
    36→    properties:
    37→      code:
    38→        const: "analog"
    39→        type: string
    40→      hasCategory:
    41→        type: object
    42→        properties:
    43→          code:
    44→            const: "measurement"
    45→            type: string
    46→          unit:
    47→            const: "volts"
    48→            type: string
    49→      voltage:
    50→        const: "5V"
    51→        type: string
    52→```
    53→
    54→## Files to Modify
    55→
    56→| File | Changes |
    57→|------|---------|
    58→| `YamlConfig.java` | Add `maxIndividualDepth` field (default: 2) |
    59→| `TaxonomyInfo.java` | Add `IndividualGraph` record for property assertions |
    60→| `SchemaVisitor.java` | Extract object/data property assertions for Pattern B |
    61→| `TaxonomyPatternMapper.java` | Generate `oneOf` with nested const objects |
    62→| `MappingContext.java` | Expose `maxIndividualDepth` from config |
    63→| `docs/configuration/config-reference.md` | Document `maxIndividualDepth` option |
    64→| `docs/ontology-design/taxonomy-patterns.md` | Update Pattern B section with new output format |
    65→
    66→## Implementation Details
    67→
    68→### 1. Configuration: YamlConfig.java
    69→
    70→**File:** `src/main/java/com/grl/component/asg/configuration/YamlConfig.java`
    71→
    72→Add new field:
    73→```java
    74→@Builder.Default
    75→private int maxIndividualDepth = 2;
    76→
    77→public int getMaxIndividualDepth() {
    78→    return maxIndividualDepth;
    79→}
    80→```
    81→
    82→### 2. Data Model: TaxonomyInfo.java
    83→
    84→**File:** `src/main/java/com/grl/component/schema/TaxonomyInfo.java`
    85→
    86→Add new record to represent individual's property graph:
    87→```java
    88→public record PropertyAssertion(
    89→    String propertyName,
    90→    @Nullable String literalValue,      // For data properties
    91→    @Nullable String literalType,       // XSD type for data properties
    92→    @Nullable OWLNamedIndividual objectValue  // For object properties
    93→) {
    94→    public boolean isDataProperty() {
    95→        return literalValue != null;
    96→    }
    97→    public boolean isObjectProperty() {
    98→        return objectValue != null;
    99→    }
   100→}
   101→
   102→public record IndividualGraph(
   103→    String code,                        // skos:notation or IRI fallback
   104→    @Nullable String displayName,       // skos:prefLabel
   105→    @Nullable String definition,        // skos:definition
   106→    List<PropertyAssertion> assertions  // object + data property assertions
   107→) {}
   108→```
   109→
   110→Update `IndividualMetadata` or replace with `IndividualGraph` in Pattern B handling.
   111→
   112→### 3. Detection: SchemaVisitor.java
   113→
   114→**File:** `src/main/java/com/grl/component/schema/SchemaVisitor.java`
   115→
   116→Modify Pattern B detection to extract property assertions:
   117→
   118→```java
   119→private IndividualGraph extractIndividualGraph(OWLNamedIndividual individual) {
   120→    String code = getCodeValue(individual);  // notation → IRI fallback
   121→    String displayName = getAnnotationValue(individual, SKOS_PREFLABEL);
   122→    String definition = getAnnotationValue(individual, SKOS_DEFINITION);
   123→
   124→    List<PropertyAssertion> assertions = new ArrayList<>();
   125→
   126→    // Extract data property assertions
   127→    EntitySearcher.getDataPropertyValues(individual, ontology)
   128→        .forEach((prop, values) -> {
   129→            for (OWLLiteral lit : values) {
   130→                assertions.add(new PropertyAssertion(
   131→                    nameResolver.resolve(prop),
   132→                    lit.getLiteral(),
   133→                    lit.getDatatype().getIRI().getShortForm(),
   134→                    null
   135→                ));
   136→            }
   137→        });
   138→
   139→    // Extract object property assertions
   140→    EntitySearcher.getObjectPropertyValues(individual, ontology)
   141→        .forEach((prop, values) -> {
   142→            for (OWLIndividual target : values) {
   143→                if (target.isNamed()) {
   144→                    assertions.add(new PropertyAssertion(
   145→                        nameResolver.resolve(prop),
   146→                        null,
   147→                        null,
   148→                        target.asOWLNamedIndividual()
   149→                    ));
   150→                }
   151→            }
   152→        });
   153→
   154→    return new IndividualGraph(code, displayName, definition, assertions);
   155→}
   156→
   157→private String getCodeValue(OWLNamedIndividual individual) {
   158→    // Fallback: skos:notation → IRI local name
   159→    String notation = getAnnotationValue(individual, SKOS_NOTATION);
   160→    return notation != null ? notation : nameResolver.resolve(individual);
   161→}
   162→```
   163→
   164→### 4. Schema Generation: TaxonomyPatternMapper.java
   165→
   166→**File:** `src/main/java/com/grl/component/mapper/TaxonomyPatternMapper.java`
   167→
   168→Replace `mapPatternB()` with graph-aware generation:
   169→
   170→```java
   171→private Schema<?> mapPatternB() {
   172→    int maxDepth = context.getMaxIndividualDepth();
   173→
   174→    // Backward compatibility: depth 0 = simple string enum (old behavior)
   175→    if (maxDepth == 0) {
   176→        return mapPatternBLegacy();
   177→    }
   178→
   179→    List<Schema<?>> individualSchemas = new ArrayList<>();
   180→    for (IndividualGraph graph : info.individualGraphs()) {
   181→        Schema<?> indSchema = buildIndividualSchema(graph, maxDepth, new HashSet<>());
   182→        individualSchemas.add(indSchema);
   183→    }
   184→
   185→    ComposedSchema oneOfSchema = new ComposedSchema();
   186→    oneOfSchema.oneOf(individualSchemas);
   187→    oneOfSchema.setDescription(description);
   188→
   189→    return wrapInArrayIfNeeded(oneOfSchema);
   190→}
   191→
   192→private Schema<?> mapPatternBLegacy() {
   193→    // Original Pattern B: simple string enum
   194→    List<String> enumValues = info.individualGraphs().stream()
   195→        .map(IndividualGraph::code)
   196→        .toList();
   197→
   198→    StringSchema enumSchema = new StringSchema();
   199→    enumSchema.setDescription(description);
   200→    enumSchema.setEnum(enumValues);
   201→
   202→    return wrapInArrayIfNeeded(enumSchema);
   203→}
   204→
   205→private Schema<?> buildIndividualSchema(IndividualGraph graph, int remainingDepth, Set<String> visited) {
   206→    ObjectSchema schema = new ObjectSchema();
   207→
   208→    // Add code field
   209→    Schema<?> codeSchema = new StringSchema();
   210→    codeSchema.setConst(graph.code());
   211→    schema.addProperty("code", codeSchema);
   212→
   213→    // Add displayName if present
   214→    if (graph.displayName() != null) {
   215→        Schema<?> displaySchema = new StringSchema();
   216→        displaySchema.setConst(graph.displayName());
   217→        schema.addProperty("displayName", displaySchema);
   218→    }
   219→
   220→    // Add definition if present
   221→    if (graph.definition() != null) {
   222→        Schema<?> defSchema = new StringSchema();
   223→        defSchema.setConst(graph.definition());
   224→        schema.addProperty("definition", defSchema);
   225→    }
   226→
   227→    // Add property assertions
   228→    for (PropertyAssertion assertion : graph.assertions()) {
   229→        Schema<?> propSchema;
   230→
   231→        if (assertion.isDataProperty()) {
   232→            propSchema = new Schema<>();
   233→            propSchema.setConst(assertion.literalValue());
   234→            propSchema.setType(mapXsdToJsonType(assertion.literalType()));
   235→        } else if (assertion.isObjectProperty() && remainingDepth > 0) {
   236→            OWLNamedIndividual target = assertion.objectValue();
   237→            String targetCode = getCodeValue(target);
   238→
   239→            if (visited.contains(targetCode)) {
   240→                // Cycle detection - use code reference
   241→                propSchema = new StringSchema();
   242→                propSchema.setConst(targetCode);
   243→            } else {
   244→                visited.add(targetCode);
   245→                IndividualGraph targetGraph = extractIndividualGraph(target);
   246→                propSchema = buildIndividualSchema(targetGraph, remainingDepth - 1, visited);
   247→                visited.remove(targetCode);
   248→            }
   249→        } else {
   250→            // Max depth reached or object property - use code reference
   251→            propSchema = new StringSchema();
   252→            propSchema.setConst(getCodeValue(assertion.objectValue()));
   253→        }
   254→
   255→        schema.addProperty(assertion.propertyName(), propSchema);
   256→    }
   257→
   258→    return schema;
   259→}
   260→```
   261→
   262→### 5. Context: MappingContext.java
   263→
   264→**File:** `src/main/java/com/grl/component/mapper/MappingContext.java`
   265→
   266→Add accessor:
   267→```java
   268→public int getMaxIndividualDepth() {
   269→    return config.getMaxIndividualDepth();
   270→}
   271→```
   272→
   273→## Documentation Updates
   274→
   275→### 1. Config Reference: docs/configuration/config-reference.md
   276→
   277→Add new section under "Optional Fields":
   278→
   279→```markdown
   280→### `maxIndividualDepth`
   281→
   282→**Type:** `integer`
   283→**Default:** `2`
   284→
   285→Controls how deeply object property assertions on Pattern B taxonomy individuals are traversed when generating `oneOf` schemas.
   286→
   287→```yaml
   288→maxIndividualDepth: 2
   289→```
   290→
   291→| Value | Behavior |
   292→|-------|----------|
   293→| `0` | Legacy mode: generates simple string enum (backward compatible) |
   294→| `1` | Single level: only direct property assertions on individuals |
   295→| `2+` | Recursive: expands nested individuals up to specified depth |
   296→
   297→**Example with depth 2:**
   298→
   299→OWL individual with nested object property:
   300→```turtle
   301→sensor:SensorTypeAnalog a sensor:SensorTypeCode ;
   302→    skos:notation "analog" ;
   303→    sensor:hasCategory sensor:MeasurementCategory .
   304→
   305→sensor:MeasurementCategory a owl:NamedIndividual ;
   306→    skos:notation "measurement" ;
   307→    sensor:unit "volts" .
   308→```
   309→
   310→Generates `oneOf` with nested objects:
   311→```yaml
   312→oneOf:
   313→  - type: object
   314→    properties:
   315→      code:
   316→        const: "analog"
   317→        type: string
   318→      hasCategory:
   319→        type: object
   320→        properties:
   321→          code:
   322→            const: "measurement"
   323→            type: string
   324→          unit:
   325→            const: "volts"
   326→            type: string
   327→```
   328→```
   329→
   330→### 2. Taxonomy Patterns: docs/ontology-design/taxonomy-patterns.md
   331→
   332→Update the Pattern B section. Replace the current "OpenAPI Output" showing simple enum with:
   333→
   334→```markdown
   335→### OpenAPI Output
   336→
   337→With `maxIndividualDepth >= 1` (default), Pattern B generates `oneOf` schemas with const objects:
   338→
   339→```yaml
   340→hasSensorType:
   341→  type: array
   342→  minItems: 1
   343→  items:
   344→    oneOf:
   345→      - type: object
   346→        properties:
   347→          code:
   348→            const: "analog"
   349→            type: string
   350→          displayName:
   351→            const: "Analog"
   352→            type: string
   353→          definition:
   354→            const: "Analog sensor type."
   355→            type: string
   356→      - type: object
   357→        properties:
   358→          code:
   359→            const: "digital"
   360→            type: string
   361→          displayName:
   362→            const: "Digital"
   363→            type: string
   364→          definition:
   365→            const: "Digital sensor type."
   366→            type: string
   367→```
   368→
   369→### Object Property Assertions
   370→
   371→When Pattern B individuals have object property assertions, these are expanded as nested objects (up to `maxIndividualDepth`):
   372→
   373→**OWL:**
   374→```xml
   375→<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeAnalog">
   376→    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/SensorTypeCode"/>
   377→    <skos:notation>analog</skos:notation>
   378→    <example:hasCategory rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementCategory"/>
   379→</owl:NamedIndividual>
   380→```
   381→
   382→**OpenAPI (depth 2):**
   383→```yaml
   384→oneOf:
   385→  - type: object
   386→    properties:
   387→      code:
   388→        const: "analog"
   389→        type: string
   390→      hasCategory:
   391→        type: object
   392→        properties:
   393→          code:
   394→            const: "measurement"
   395→            type: string
   396→```
   397→
   398→### Cycle Detection
   399→
   400→If object property assertions form a cycle (A → B → A), ASG detects this and uses a string reference instead of infinite nesting.
   401→
   402→### Legacy Mode
   403→
   404→Set `maxIndividualDepth: 0` to generate simple string enums (pre-enhancement behavior):
   405→
   406→```yaml
   407→hasSensorType:
   408→  type: array
   409→  minItems: 1
   410→  items:
   411→    type: string
   412→    enum:
   413→      - analog
   414→      - digital
   415→```
   416→```
   417→
   418→Also update the Pattern Overview table at the top:
   419→
   420→```markdown
   421→| Pattern | OWL Structure | OpenAPI Output | Use Case |
   422→|---------|---------------|----------------|----------|
   423→| **A** | `owl:oneOf` with untyped individuals | Inline string enum | Simple, closed enumeration |
   424→| **B** | `owl:oneOf` with typed individuals | `oneOf` with const objects | Closed enumeration with graphs |
   425→| **C** | Named class (no SKOS) | `$ref` to schema | Extensible, hierarchical taxonomy |
   426→| **D** | SKOS-based class | Inline string enum | Standards-compliant vocabulary |
   427→```
   428→
   429→Update the Pattern A vs B Comparison table:
   430→
   431→```markdown
   432→| Aspect | Pattern A | Pattern B |
   433→|--------|-----------|-----------|
   434→| Individual typing | `owl:NamedIndividual` only | Domain class + metadata |
   435→| OpenAPI output | Simple string enum | `oneOf` with const objects |
   436→| Property assertions | Not supported | Expanded as nested objects |
   437→| SPARQL query | Parse `owl:oneOf` | `?x a SensorTypeCode` |
   438→| Metadata support | `rdfs:label` only | Full annotation + property support |
   439→```
   440→

   484→## Verification

   497→## Backward Compatibility
   498→
   499→- `maxIndividualDepth: 0` preserves old behavior: generates simple `enum: ['analog', 'digital']`
   500→- `maxIndividualDepth: 1+` generates `oneOf` with const objects
   501→- Default `maxIndividualDepth: 2` is a **breaking change** for existing Pattern B users
   502→- Document migration path in release notes
   503→
   504→## Metadata Handling
   505→
   506→When `maxIndividualDepth >= 1`, include `displayName` and `definition` in const objects if annotations are present:

   514→        type: string

   517→        type: string
   518→      definition:
   519→        const: "Analog sensor type"
   520→        type: string

   522→        # ... nested object
   523→```
